title: Mock CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog (offline demo sample)
note: This is a small, hand-picked sample of real, publicly documented CVEs used ONLY so the pipeline can run end-to-end without network access. It is not a live feed.

vulnerabilities.vulnerabilityName: Apache Log4j2 Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.shortDescription: Apache Log4j2 JNDI features used in configuration, log messages, and parameters do not protect against attacker-controlled LDAP and other JNDI-related endpoints, enabling remote code execution.
vulnerabilities.requiredAction: Apply updates per vendor instructions or mitigate with documented workarounds.

vulnerabilities.vulnerabilityName: Microsoft Windows SMB Remote Code Execution Vulnerability (EternalBlue)
vulnerabilities.shortDescription: The SMBv1 server in Microsoft Windows allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted packets, exploited by WannaCry and NotPetya.
vulnerabilities.requiredAction: Apply updates per vendor instructions or disable SMBv1.

vulnerabilities.vulnerabilityName: Microsoft Windows Print Spooler Remote Code Execution Vulnerability (PrintNightmare)
vulnerabilities.shortDescription: Windows Print Spooler service improperly performs privileged file operations, allowing a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code with SYSTEM privileges.
vulnerabilities.requiredAction: Apply updates per vendor instructions or disable the Print Spooler service on servers that do not require it.

vulnerabilities.vulnerabilityName: Microsoft Exchange Server Remote Code Execution Vulnerability (ProxyLogon SSRF)
vulnerabilities.shortDescription: Microsoft Exchange Server allows an attacker to send arbitrary HTTP requests and authenticate as the Exchange server (SSRF), the first stage of the ProxyLogon exploit chain.
